notes: Closed-loop cooling. One-time 1M gallon fill per 200MW building. Annual replenishment of 20% (~1.2M gal/yr at full scale). Recurring 400,000-640,000 gal/yr for staff and process support. Total ~1.2M gal/yr replenishment = ~3,288 gal/day. Comparable to a restaurant or office building.

CloudBurst 1.2GW campus. Estimated up to 70,000 gallons per day (~25 million gallons per year). Company said it would recycle water. Exact source and volume still under determination at time of reporting.

Wildcat Ridge Data Center Campus (Kriger-connected via Five Up Realty/Marzolino). Average 598,000 gal/day with daily maximum of 3,310,149 gallons. Variation due to seasonal cooling demands. Exploring mine water as supplemental cooling source.

Proposed 1500MW data center on 209 acres in residential zone. Would have drawn up to 300,000 gallons per day from local water supply. Developer withdrew proposal after massive community opposition (~500 attendees at meeting).

AWS 'Project Green' near New Florence. Each of 17 buildings uses ~2.9M gal/yr. Full buildout: ~50M gal/yr (~137,000 gal/day). Water district manager estimated ~100,000 gal/day average. Outside air used for cooling ~93% of the year; water only during hottest conditions. 60% more water efficient than industry average.

Stonebridge 1.2GW data center campus. Closed-loop cooling system. Approximately 50,000 gallons per day. Developer claims 75% less water than older data centers, comparable to a local Wawa convenience store.

Highlander SM One LLC proposal (related to CloudBurst). Closed-loop, non-evaporative system. Up to 75,000 gallons per day, representing ~1% of San Marcos total daily water usage. City Council voted 5-2 to deny the proposal.

Google 700MW data center. Expected to use 50,000 gallons daily, equivalent to ~160 homes. Closed-loop system with high-efficiency heat pumps. Water from WLSSD (not groundwater). Significantly lower than many comparable data centers.

QTS $2B data center. Water-free cooling system (no water for cooling). Maximum daily usage ~18,000 gallons for domestic/operational needs only. One-time fill of ~4 Olympic pools for cooling system. Wastewater: 9,200 gal/day.

Meta/Balloonist $1.6B campus on 320 acres. Closed-loop system. ~75,000 gallons per day of municipal water and wastewater. Would be 5th-highest water user in Menomonie. Project paused/delayed as of late 2025.

Trammell Crow White Oak Technology Park. 29-building campus on 1,945 acres. Worst-case scenario from rezoning packet: 6,598,000 gal/day water consumption with 3,958,000 gal/day sewage. No end-user secured yet so actual cooling system unknown. Could take a decade to complete.
Monarch Energy $12B data center on 1,100 acres near Chicago-Rockford International Airport. Needs 3 million gallons of water per day and 700MW of power.

AWS $12B across three campuses. Shreveport site alone: 7.5M gal/day potable water with 2.5M gal/day wastewater returned. Bossier City: 1.8-13.5M gal/day. Blanchard/Caddo: 1.5-5M gal/day. Water used for cooling <13% of year. AWS investing $400M in public water infrastructure.

AWS contractual water purchase: must purchase 7M gal/day from City of Wilmington regardless of actual usage (helps city meet existing reservoir contract). Uses direct evaporative cooling. Peak water use expected 'less than a month out of the year.' City water plant permitted for up to 4M gal/day, currently produces 2.2-2.8M gal/day. Note: 7M is contractual purchase, not confirmed consumption.

Two data center projects in Hanover: (1) HHHunt Hunting Hawk near Wyndham: ~10,500 gal/day (planning commission rejected); (2) Tract Mountain Road Technology Park 430-acre campus: average 600,000 gal/day, peak 2M gal/day. The 600,000 figure is for the larger Tract project which is the active proposal.
